Off Calendar Oct-03-2024 Continued To Nov-20-24 At 10:30 A.M. In Dept 610

DEMURRER to Amended COMPLAINT
Off Calendar Sep-04-2024 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES:
No appearances. The Court adopts the tentative ruling as follows:

DEFENDANT NEW CENTURY CUSTOM CABINETS' DEMURRER to 1ST Amended COMPLAINT.

Defendant's demurrer to the first amended complaint (FAC) is overruled. The demurrer says the FAC is "barred by the statute of limitation and the delayed discovery rule does not apply." (Dmr. Not. 2:6-7.) However, the FAC adequately pleads the delayed discovery rule, and it is a factual issue that can be explored in discovery. (Id. at 3:25-4:13; Fox v. Ethicon Endo-Surgery, Inc. (2005) 35 Cal.4th 797, 807, 810.) To the extent the demurrer separately attacks the fraud count on specificity grounds, that count is adequately pled. (See, e.g., FAC 3:12-15.)

Counsel for the prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order which repeats verbatim the substantive portion of the Court's ruling.

Judge: Richard B. Ulmer; Clerk: W. Trupek; Not Reported (302/RBU)

DEMURRER to Amended COMPLAINT
Off Calendar Aug-20-2024 Continued To Sep-04-24 At 09:30 A.M. In Dept 302
MINI MINUTES:
Appearances via Zoom Conference
Jay Y Baik, Esq., Law Office of Majeed Samara, 650-588-7700 Jaybaiklaw@gmail.com Attorney for Plaintiff Conliza Pang
Arthur Liu, Esq., Inter-Pacific Law Group, Inc. 510-986-1198 arthurliussa@yahoo.com for Defendnat New Centruy Custom Cabinet

Discussed, Not ARGUED. Court makes an inquiry re: Plaintiff's untimely filed opposition. Plaintiff makes a request for continuance to allow consideration of Plaintiff's opposition. Counsel for defendant does not oppose Plaintiff's request.

The Court modifies the tentative ruling as follows:

DEFENDANT NEW CENTURY CUSTOM CABINETS' DEMURRER to Amended COMPLAINT is continued to September 4, 2024 at 9:30 a.m. Dept. 302. Parties are reminded that compliance with SFLR 2.7(b) is required. Court indicates to counsel that the Court is in receipt of the moving papers for this motion only. The Court does have courtesy copies of a reply or opposition. Any new filings are to reflect the new hearing date of September 4, 2024.

Judge: Richard B. Ulmer; Clerk: W. Trupek; Not Reported (302/RBU)

MINI MINUTES:

Hearing Re: DEFENDANT NEW CENTURY CUSTOM CABINETS' DEMURRER to Amended COMPLAINT.

No appearances made in this matter. The Court adopts its tentative ruling.

Off calendar. The parties failed to comply with the meet and confer requirement of CCP 430.41. (See Liu Decl.) The parties are ordered to comply with the code. Movant's response to the amended complaint is now due by March 20, 2024.

Judge: Richard B. Ulmer Jr.; Clerk: V. Da Fonseca; Not Reported. (302/RBU)

Off Calendar Jan-17-2024 Continued To Feb-20-24 At 09:30 A.M. In Dept 302

APPEARANCES:
Atty. KEVIN TAHENY, Esq. "specially appearing" for atty. Majeed S. Samara (Tel: 650-588-7700) for plaintiff (via Zoom videoconference);
Atty. ARTHUR J. LIU, Esq. of Inter-Pacific Law Group Inc. (Tel: 510-986-1198) for defendant New Century Custom Cabinets.

HEARING RE: DEFENDANT NEW CENTURY CUSTOM CABINETS' DEMURRER TO FIRST AMENDED COMPLAINT.

The Court notes plaintiff's opposition was filed one day late.

The Court orders counsel for parties to meet and confer in compliance with CCP 430.41.

The Court continues the hearing on the demurrer to February 20, 2024 at 9:30 a.m. in Dept. 302, Law & Motion.
